CREATE TABLE test_table (input_value String, default_value Int64);
INSERT INTO test_table VALUES ('123', CAST('10', 'Int64')), ('abc', CAST('10', 'Int64')), ('-200', CAST('5', 'Int64')), ('0xc0fe', CAST('-1', 'Int64')), ('NaN', CAST('0', 'Int64'));
SELECT input_value, toInt64OrDefault(input_value, default_value) AS converted_value FROM test_table;
SELECT toInt64OrDefault('256', 100) AS result_1, toInt64OrDefault('abc', 100) AS result_2 FROM test_table LIMIT 1;
WITH (SELECT CAST('5.7' AS String)) AS input, (SELECT CAST('0' AS Int64)) AS default_value SELECT toInt64OrDefault(input, default_value) AS result;
SELECT toInt64OrDefault(input_value, default_value) AS result FROM (SELECT input_value, default_value FROM test_table) AS subquery;
CREATE TABLE another_test_table (col1 Int64, col2 Int64) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY col1;
SELECT a.col1, toInt64OrDefault(CAST(b.col2 AS String), 0) AS converted_col2 FROM another_test_table AS a JOIN another_test_table AS b ON a.col1 = b.col1 + 1;
SELECT toInt64OrDefault('Inf', CAST('20', 'Int64')) AS infinity_test, toInt64OrDefault('-Inf', CAST('30', 'Int64')) AS negative_infinity_test;
